kernel_5_op.intake: hive raw 66 = ai-native-error convention: failure path emits `reason=<slug> fix=<directive>` to stderr. Sweep nexus/tool/*.hexa + design/kick/*.json, classify, propose convention refinements.

fanout_axes.method: grep eprintln+reason= in tool/*.hexa AND grep "reason= / raw_66 in design/kick/*.json
3-level grading: excellent (copy-pasteable cmd) / good (clear next-step / inspect-this-file) / vague (verify foo, check bar without literal cmd)

kernel_5_op.tier1_gate: F1+F2+F3 PASS for inventory honesty; F4+F5 PASS for proposal

deviations_missing_fix_directive_or_conf.deviation: sentinel-line-only-no-fix — fix is on the FOLLOWING line (940). Two-line pattern (sentinel without fix, then bare reason+fix). Acceptable per dual-channel convention but not single-trailer-uniform.
minimally-actionable — `see --help` is borderline vague vs good; should be `hexa run tool/signals_sync.hexa --help`

embedded_in_design_kick_witnesses_summar.raw_66_field_present_in_witness_body: 30 of 52 witnesses mention raw 66 (typically as raw_66_ai_native_error_message field, decision-rationale references, or copy-of-prior-trailer)
examples_of_well_formed_witness_raw_66_f: design/kick/2026-04-27_omega-cycle-quality-audit-session_omega_cycle.json: raw_66_ai_native_error_message: "no error this cycle; reason+fix trailer reserved for failure path" (correct null-case handling)

excellent.definition: Trailer fix= is a literal copy-pasteable command (hexa run ... / ls / tail / mkdir / env-var-prefix-of-rerun) OR a literal flag spec (--foo {a|b}) executable with zero translation. Includes inline placeholders like <abs_path> that are obvious to fill.

good.definition: Trailer fix= is a clear actionable next-step but not a literal cmd: 'verify <abs_path> exists' (implicit ls), 'inspect .bak.<ts> files and re-run --selftest', 're-read tool/kick_bench.hexa:488-496'. Operator knows exactly which file/function to touch even though no cmd is spelled out.

vague.definition: Trailer fix= says 'verify X' or 'adjust X' or 'check X' without naming a literal artifact, OR 'schedule kick(ω-cycle ...)' which defers all action to a future cycle without unblocking the operator. Or 'see --help' style bare-reference. Internal selftest assertions ('verify _slugify_cycle mirrors ingest') count as vague when they require deep code reading.

literal_pattern_reason_eq_slug_space_fix.edge_unknown_arg_format: 5 sites use `reason=unknown-arg-<a>` or `reason=batch-missing-arg`; embedded user-supplied <a> can introduce spaces/special chars but slug remains kebab-prefixed (acceptable variant — flag for future hardening)
literal_pattern_reason_eq_slug_space_fix.share: 94.5% (86/91) emit a single-line raw 66 trailer; 7 emit dual-channel (sentinel line + raw 66 line)

slug_quality_kebab_case_ascii_no_spaces.note: Every observed slug is kebab-case ASCII. Some embed user-supplied tokens (`unknown-arg-<a>`) which could leak non-kebab chars at runtime, but the static slug template is compliant.

top_5_deviations_verbatim.issue: vague — `verify <fn> mirrors <other>` requires deep code-read; no literal diff/grep cmd

top_5_deviations_verbatim.verbatim: eprintln("  - " + f + " // reason=expectation-not-met fix=adjust formula or fixture")
top_5_deviations_verbatim.issue: vague — `adjust formula or fixture` names neither file nor target value

top_5_deviations_verbatim.verbatim: eprintln("__SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY__ FAIL applied=0 patches=0 skipped=0 reason=patch-out-missing-arg")
top_5_deviations_verbatim.issue: dual-channel — sentinel line carries reason but no fix; fix arrives on the next eprintln. Single-line consumers (grep+parse) miss the fix unless they also read the next line.

top_5_deviations_verbatim.verbatim: "raw_66": "reason=greenfield-no-bench-jsonl-decoder fix=schedule kick(ω-cycle build-bench-jsonl-status-aware-decoder; consume state/kick/bench/2026-04-27_synthetic-fail-inner-rc-decoder-smoke.jsonl as smoke fixture"
top_5_deviations_verbatim.issue: schedule-another-cycle — defers all action to a future cycle; missing closing paren; no immediate operator unblock
minimally-actionable — `see --help` vs sister tools that emit `fix=hexa run tool/<X>.hexa --help`. Inconsistent uniformity with the more-explicit sites in the same codebase.

convention_proposal_raw_66_v2.rationale: Sweep shows 94.5% single-line literal-pattern compliance and 100% kebab-slug compliance. The leftover 5–6% deviation buckets are: dual-channel sentinel-without-fix (7 sites, all in signals_sync.hexa); vague `verify <fn>` family (~14 sites in classifier/slugify selftests); and 1 schedule-deferred witness trailer. The proposal preserves all current conventions and only adds 4 narrow refinements:

additions.rule: When a tool emits both a sentinel (`__FOO__ FAIL ...`) line and a raw 66 trailer, the trailer MUST be a single self-contained line carrying both reason= and fix=. The sentinel may carry reason= for grep-friendliness but MUST NOT be the only line carrying it. (Effect: signals_sync.hexa 7 sites become uniform with the rest of nexus tools.)
additions.delta_from_today: Today some sentinel lines carry reason= without fix=; the fix arrives on the next eprintln. New rule: paired emission is fine, but each line independently parseable.

additions.rule: fix= directive MUST be at minimum 'good' tier per the 3-level rubric (literal cmd OR clear inspect-this-file/function pointer). 'vague' tier (verify <fn> mirrors <other>) is permitted ONLY for selftest-internal assertions where the audience is an implementor reading the source, not an operator at the CLI. Such selftest-only trailers SHOULD be wrapped in a `# selftest:` prefix to mark audience.
additions.delta_from_today: ~14 vague trailers in atlas_absorb_lint/sweeper/hook + witness_emit selftests would gain a `# selftest:` prefix in their reason line.

additions.rule: When fix= directs to a tool's help, prefer `fix=hexa run tool/<X>.hexa --help` over `fix=see --help`. The full path makes the directive copy-pasteable from any cwd.

When the only available fix is to schedule a downstream ω-cycle, the trailer SHOULD additionally include an immediate-unblock directive (e.g. `--skip-decoder` flag, or pointer to a stub fixture). Pure `fix=schedule kick(ω-cycle ...)` is acceptable in witness JSON raw_66 fields but discouraged for live stderr trailers since it leaves the current operator with no immediate next step.

non_changes_explicit: trailing-context fields after fix= (`raw="..." default=N`) — keep (good for diagnostics, doesn't break parser)
embedded user-supplied tokens via `unknown-arg-<a>` — keep but document that runtime slug may contain non-kebab chars
carry_forward_kicks: kick(ω-cycle signals-sync-trailer-uniformity-fix) — apply R1 to 7 sites in tool/signals_sync.hexa
kick(ω-cycle selftest-trailer-prefix) — apply R2 to ~14 selftest sites in tool/atlas_absorb_*.hexa + tool/witness_emit.hexa
kick(ω-cycle tool-help-uniformity) — apply R3 to tool/signals_sync.hexa:970

F1_sweep_finds_all_trailers.evidence: 97 hits in tool/*.hexa eprintln; 6 excluded as documentation/struct-field; 91 net trailers. 7 literal reason= strings + 30 raw_66 mentions in design/kick/*.json. Coverage spans 41 tool files and 30 witness files.

F2_each_classified.evidence: Per-trailer table groups all 91 tool trailers + 7 witness trailers into 4 buckets: excellent (55), good (18), vague (18), missing-fix-deviation (8). Sum verified.

F3_deviations_flagged_honestly.evidence: 8 deviations called out verbatim with file:line, including dual-channel signals_sync sites and the host-path leak in live-spawn-end-to-end witness. No paint-over.

F4_actionability_rubric_explicit_3_level.evidence: Rubric defines excellent/good/vague with literal definitions and counts (60% / 20% / 20%).

F5_convention_proposal_justified.evidence: 4 additions (R1-R4) each map to ≥1 observed deviation with file:line. Non-changes explicit. Carry-forward kicks named.

tier1_gate_result: PASS (5/5 falsifiers; F1+F2+F3 inventory-honest; F4+F5 proposal-justified)
